title: BNCI 2015-013 Error-Related Potentials dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 6 healthy subjects performing an error-related potential (ErrP) monitoring task. Subjects observed a cursor moving autonomously toward targets with 20-40% error probability while maintaining fixation, without direct control over cursor movement. The study demonstrates that error-related brain potentials can be detected and decoded during passive monitoring of external agents, providing foundational evidence for error detection in brain-computer interface applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired from 64 channels using a Biosemi ActiveTwo system at 512 Hz sampling rate with standard 10-20 montage. Subjects performed 20 sessions each, with 10 blocks per session. Each block contained approximately 50-64 trials with 2-second trial duration. Subjects monitored a visual cursor (green square) moving toward targets (blue for left, red for right) with no active control, only passive assessment of cursor performance.
